variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
# complete skincare starter guide you're at 94kg and 23, so skin quality matters for your overall look. the evidence is clear: most beginners fail because they stack too many products at once, not because they lack them.

start with barrier repair first, then add one active later. --- ## key principles before you start **one active at a time.** don't mix acids + retinoids + vitamin c. pick one, master it, add the next in 4–6 weeks. **SPF is non-negotiable.** UV damage worsens acne, pigmentation, and collagen breakdown. **barrier health comes first.** if your skin stings on basic products, pause actives and repair with ceramides + panthenol before anything else. ---  ---  ---  ---  --- ## what to avoid in week 1 don't exfoliate, don't use acids, don't layer multiple actives.

if your skin stings on the basic cleanser or moisturizer, your barrier is already compromised. pause everything except the gentle cleanser and ceramide-rich moisturizer until stinging stops (usually 1–2 weeks).

your wake time is 07:00 and sleep is 23:00, so apply SPF right after your 07:00 shower and moisturizer. retinoid (if you go that route) goes on at night, 30 min after cleansing, 2–3x per week to start.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Anthelios Melt-in Milk SPF 50+
products.description: High-protection broad-spectrum SPF 50 with cosmetic-elegant feel.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

visual_blocks.title: starter product stack by skin type

rows: lightweight on t-zone, richer on cheeks

steps.label: week 1–2: foundation
steps.detail: cleanse AM + PM with gentle cleanser.  apply moisturizer + ceramides/panthenol. no actives yet.  goal: establish routine, assess barrier health.

steps.label: week 3–4: add SPF
steps.detail: introduce daily SPF in AM after moisturizer. continue cleanse + moisturize.  watch for stinging or excessive dryness; if present, pause and repair barrier longer.

steps.label: week 5–6: first active
steps.detail: if skin is calm, add one active: azelaic AM (oily/acne) or adapalene PM (all types). use 2–3x per week, build to daily over 2 weeks.  keep moisturizer + SPF constant.

steps.label: week 7–8: assess & adjust
steps.detail: evaluate skin response. if clear and calm, maintain routine.  if irritated, drop active frequency or pause. do not stack a second active yet.

items: pick your skin type (oily/acne, dry/sensitive, or combo) and grab the matching cleanser + moisturizer
buy ceramides + panthenol product (CeraVe moisturizing cream or La Roche-Posay cicaplast balm)
get a daily SPF 30+ (EltaMD UV clear or La Roche-Posay anthelios)
week 1: cleanse AM + PM, moisturize AM + PM, no actives
week 3: add SPF to AM routine after moisturizer
week 5: introduce one active (azelaic or adapalene) at low frequency, 2–3x per week
week 7: assess skin; if calm, maintain; if irritated, dial back active frequency
do not stack two actives in the first 8 weeks

cards.label: active at a time
cards.hint: stacking acids + retinoids kills absorption and damages barrier

cards.label: minimum to assess routine
cards.hint: skin cycles every 4 weeks; give changes time to show

cards.label: non-negotiable: SPF daily
cards.hint: UV is the #1 driver of aging, pigmentation, and acne worsening

cards.label: exfoliants in week 1
cards.hint: barrier repair first; actives come after
